behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename detection plus CLI categorization. A mocked diff can miss real subprocess/output-shape issues (e.g., rename status format/flags), so minimum is System. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is purely git/CLI reporting, with no AI/ML step.
behaviors.test_description:
Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI via subprocess, and assert report shows 1 rename and no extra add/delete for that file.
Pass 1 (INTENT TAG): real-infra
Pass 2 (LABEL MAP): real-infra->System

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Crash risk comes from real git binary diff output and subprocess decoding/handling. Mocks can hide encoding and process-output edge cases. Rejected higher levels: no model output involved in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description:
In a real temp repo, commit then modify/add a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run CLI as subprocess, assert exit success and binary file is counted in report.
Pass 1 (INTENT TAG): real-infra
Pass 2 (LABEL MAP): real-infra->System

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real git behavior for an unborn branch/no-commit repo, which is subprocess/OS-level behavior that mocks frequently misrepresent. Rejected higher levels: behavior is CLI/git error handling only, no AI/ML.
behaviors.test_description:
Initialize a real git repo with zero commits, run the CLI subprocess, assert no crash/error and structured report is empty.
Pass 1 (INTENT TAG): real-infra
Pass 2 (LABEL MAP): real-infra->System

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against actual git history; requires real repository state plus CLI subprocess execution to catch true mismatches. Integration stubs can hide history/query mismatches. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no AI/ML decision step.
behaviors.test_description:
Build a real repo history with known adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run CLI subprocess on that repo, and assert reported category counts equal expected history-derived counts.
Pass 1 (INTENT TAG): real-infra
Pass 2 (LABEL MAP): real-infra->System

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real git index vs working-tree semantics and how CLI invokes git commands; this is a subprocess/infrastructure boundary. Mocks often collapse staged/unstaged distinctions and can miss real failures. Rejected higher levels: no model-output or multi-agent pipeline in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description:
In a real repo, create equivalent change sets once as staged and once as unstaged, run CLI subprocess for each scenario, and assert structured outputs are identical.
Pass 1 (INTENT TAG): real-infra
Pass 2 (LABEL MAP): real-infra->System
